context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the metadata extraction pattern from the namespace filter module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The code should apply the same logic used elsewhere to retrieve and compare metadata fields (specifically looking for 'source', 'type', or 'group' keys based on existing patterns in the codebase)

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the sentinel value defined in the core group constants file
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This constraint requires finding the specific magic hex string (currently hidden) that represents an empty or internal core group, likely located in a constant file named 'core_groups.py' or similar.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_namespace_refresh_from_lists..description: Refresh namespace membership from listed namespace objects, adding matching names, preserving prior matches across incremental refreshes, removing deletion-marked names, and ignoring names outside configured patterns.

feature1_2_namespace_refresh_from_events.description: Refresh namespace membership from namespace event objects, treating ordinary events as additions, deletion timestamps or deletion event types as removals, and ignoring names outside configured patterns.

feature2_namespace_watch_event_processin.description: Apply a single namespace watch event, ignoring initial-list events without notification and notifying when follow-up events add or remove a matching namespace.

feature3_1_resource_refresh_by_selectors.description: Refresh served resources for configured workload reactions, ignoring admission-only reactions, adding matching watchable resources, and replacing either the whole catalog or one API group depending on the refresh scope.

feature3_2_resource_refresh_ambiguous_an.description: Resolve broad resource selectors against discovered resources, excluding ambiguous specific matches, preferring the core API when a plain name collides with an extension API, allowing explicit catch-all selection, and warning when no resource matches.

feature3_3_resource_capability_filtering.description: Filter selected resources by required API capabilities, rejecting resources without list/watch support and rejecting non-patchable resources when the configured reaction needs persistent state updates.

feature4_resource_definition_event_proce.description: Apply one resource-definition watch event, ignoring initial-list events, rescanning the affected API group for follow-up changes, updating served resources from API discovery, handling disappeared groups as empty, and filling core backbone resources from core API discovery.

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'refresh_namespaces'
_constraints.output_rule: Triggers namespace name state updates based on source and event types

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'apply_namespace_event'
_constraints.output_rule: Triggers notification signaling and state updates for watch events

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'refresh_resources'
_constraints.output_rule: Triggers API resource state updates including filtering and grouping logic

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'apply_resource_definition_event'
_constraints.output_rule: Triggers event processing with scanner and core backbone logic

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'refresh_namespaces' and (input.steps[x].source == 'bodies' or input.steps[x].source == 'events')
_constraints.output_rule: Determines processing logic based on source: bodies = metadata comparison, events = type + metadata comparison

_constraints.input_condition: Step source is 'bodies' or 'events'
_constraints.output_rule: Only items where metadata.name matches at least one pattern in input.patterns are added or removed from state

_constraints.input_condition: Step source is 'bodies' or 'events' and metadata is present with deletionTimestamp
_constraints.output_rule: Namespace is removed from state (output set becomes empty if no other matches exist)

_constraints.input_condition: Step source is 'events' and event.type is present
_constraints.output_rule: Event type 'DELETED' causes removal regardless of deletionTimestamp; 'ADDED' or 'MODIFIED' causes addition

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'apply_namespace_event' and step event.type is null
_constraints.output_rule: Type null represents initial list; 'notified' is set to 'no'

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'apply_namespace_event' and step event.type is 'ADDED' or 'MODIFIED'
_constraints.output_rule: 'notified' is set to 'yes' and name is added to state

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'apply_namespace_event' and step event.type is not null
_constraints.output_rule: 'notified' is always 'yes' when event.type is present

_constraints.input_condition: input.action == 'refresh_resources'
_constraints.output_rule: admission_validation and watch_event modes filter resources by verbs; background_task mode rejects unreadable resources

_constraints.input_condition: handler_mode is 'admission_validation' or 'watch_event'
_constraints.output_rule: Resources are only included if they have 'list' and 'watch' in their verbs array

_constraints.input_condition: handler_mode is 'background_task' and name mode requires updates
_constraints.output_rule: Resources are excluded if they do not have 'patch' in verbs array

_constraints.input_condition: handler_mode is 'admission_validation' and group is present
_constraints.output_rule: Only 'group' matches are considered; other group resources are ignored

_constraints.input_condition: input.selector.plural matches the verb of resources
_constraints.output_rule: Resources with 'list' and 'watch' in verbs are included

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple resources have the same plural but different groups, and no explicit selector group is set
_constraints.output_rule: Warning 'ambiguous_resource' is printed; no resources are served

_constraints.input_condition: Selector plural matches resource plural, selector group is null, and core group is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Core backbone resource (group is empty) is preferred over extension resources

_constraints.input_condition: input.selector is 'all'
_constraints.output_rule: All discovered resources are considered for matching

_constraints.input_condition: handler_mode is 'watch_event'
_constraints.output_rule: Group/plural match is required; type must be present

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'apply_resource_definition_event' and event.type is null
_constraints.output_rule: Initial list event; no scanner triggered, state not updated

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'apply_resource_definition_event' and event.type is not null
_constraints.output_rule: Event type present triggers scanner on affected API group

_constraints.input_condition: Action is 'apply_resource_definition_event' and event.type matches group
_constraints.output_rule: Core backbone resources (group is null/empty) are filled separately, not merged with API discovered resources in the same output line

_constraints.input_condition: output is namespace list
_constraints.output_rule: Names are comma-separated at the same level as the first name

_constraints.input_condition: Action equals 'apply_namespace_event'
_constraints.output_rule: Output line: 'notified=<yes|no>' followed by 'namespaces=<list>'

_constraints.input_condition: Action equals 'refresh_resources' or 'apply_resource_definition_event'
_constraints.output_rule: Output line: 'resources=<comma-separated list>'

_constraints.input_condition: report_warnings is true and specific condition met
_constraints.output_rule: Warning printed on same line as resources: 'resources=<list>warnings=<warning>'
